Wisconsin · State Licensing Reference

Speech-Language Pathologist
Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP) Guide

Issued by the Examining Board of Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists under the Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services (DSPS). Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Telepractice Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$78
$78 application and license fee
Renewal
$78
Every 2 years (February 28)
Processing Time
2 to 4 weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Examining Board of Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists

Mailing Address

P.O. Box 8935, Madison, WI 53708-8935

Physical Address

4822 Madison Yards Way, Madison, WI 53705

Phone & Fax

Phone: (608) 266-2112

Fax: (608) 267-0644

Email

dsps@wisconsin.gov

Credential Categories

Wisconsin SLP License Types

Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist

Full credential for independent SLP practice in Wisconsin.

Provisional SLP License

For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Wisconsin Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP)

Earn a Master's Degree in SLP

From a CAA-accredited program.

Pass the Praxis Examination

Test code 5331, score 162.

Complete Clinical Fellowship

36 weeks supervised clinical practice.

Submit Application to DSPS

Submit with $78 fee.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official graduate transcriptsFrom a CAA-accredited program.
  • Praxis exam score reportScore of 162 or higher.
  • Clinical Fellowship verificationCF completion documentation.
  • Background checkCriminal background check.
Telepractice

Wisconsin Telepractice Rules for SLPs

Telehealth Practice

Wisconsin permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Clinical Fellowship & Provisional Rules

Wisconsin issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Wisconsin is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

Frequently Asked Questions

Wisconsin SLP FAQ

How much does a Wisconsin SLP license cost?

$78 initial. $78 biennial renewal.

Is Wisconsin part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

Yes.

Does Wisconsin allow telepractice for SLPs?

Yes.

Independent, YMYL-compliant licensing guides for speech-language pathologists across the United States.
